devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । अथ तस्य पुनर्विप्रैः अपुत्रस्य महीपतेः । बाहुभ्यां मथ्यमानाभ्यां मिथुनं समपद्यत ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaatha tasya punarvipraiḥ aputrasya mahīpateḥbāhubhyāṃ mathyamānābhyāṃ mithunaṃ samapadyata
translation:
Maitreya said:
Thereupon, a couple (a boy and a girl) was born from the arms of the sonless king, when they (the arms) were being churned by the Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: तद् दृष्ट्वा मिथुनं जातं ऋषयो ब्रह्मवादिनः । ऊचुः परमसन्तुष्टा विदित्वा भगवत्कलाम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tad dṛṣṭvā mithunaṃ jātaṃ ṛṣayo brahmavādinaḥūcuḥ paramasantuṣṭā viditvā bhagavatkalām
translation: Having seen the birth of the couple, the sages who were well-versed in the Vedas, became highly pleased to know that it was an aṃśa (portion or digit) of the glorious Lord and spoke.

devanagari: ऋषय ऊचुः । एष विष्णोर्भगवतः कला भुवनपालिनी । इयं च लक्ष्म्याः सम्भूतिः पुरुषस्यानपायिनी ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣaya ūcuḥeṣa viṣṇorbhagavataḥ kalā bhuvanapālinīiyaṃ ca lakṣmyāḥ sambhūtiḥ puruṣasyānapāyinī
translation:
Sages said:
This (Pṛthu) is a manifestation of that aṃśa (portion) of Lord Viṣṇu which protects the world[1]. This (girl) is the portion and incarnation of Goddess Lakṣmī who is never disunited from the Supreme Man (Viṣṇu)[2].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Viṣṇoḥ kalā: (i) The special āveśa type of avatāra—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Who was filled with the presence of Viṣṇu.—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [2]:
Here ends Ch. 14 of the Bh. P. as per Padaratnāvalī’s text. Some texts omit ses 2 and 3.

devanagari: अयं तु प्रथमो राज्ञां पुमान् प्रथयिता यशः । पृथुर्नाम महाराजो भविष्यति पृथुश्रवाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ tu prathamo rājñāṃ pumān prathayitā yaśaḥpṛthurnāma mahārājo bhaviṣyati pṛthuśravāḥ
translation: As to this male child, he will be the first and foremost among kings, the enhancer of their glory. As his fame will spread far and wide, he will be known as Pṛthu, the great king.

devanagari: इयं च सुदती देवी गुणभूषणभूषणा । अर्चिर्नाम वरारोहा पृथुमेवावरुन्धती ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: iyaṃ ca sudatī devī guṇabhūṣaṇabhūṣaṇāarcirnāma varārohā pṛthumevāvarundhatī
translation: And this beautiful, resplendent lady with finely set teeth who is the ornament of ornaments and virtues, will be called Arcis and will be Pṛthu’s consort.

devanagari: एष साक्षात् हरेरंशो जातो लोकरिरक्षया । इयं च तत्परा हि श्रीः अनुजज्ञेऽनपायिनी ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa sākṣāt hareraṃśo jāto lokarirakṣayāiyaṃ ca tatparā hi śrīḥ anujajñe'napāyinī
translation: It is with the object of protecting the world that this aṃśa (portion) of direct Hari himself, is born. And this is verily the Goddess Lakṣmī who is inseparable from him and ever (absolutely) devoted to him.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । प्रशंसन्ति स्म तं विप्रा गन्धर्वप्रवरा जगुः । मुमुचुः सुमनोधाराः सिद्धा नृत्यन्ति स्वःस्त्रियः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācapraśaṃsanti sma taṃ viprā gandharvapravarā jaguḥmumucuḥ sumanodhārāḥ siddhā nṛtyanti svaḥstriyaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Brāhmaṇas eulogised him; the foremost among the Gandharvas sang (his glory); Siddhas poured down showers of (heavenly) flowers; the celestial damsels danced.

devanagari: शङ्खतूर्यमृदङ्गाद्या नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो दिवि । तत्र सर्व उपाजग्मुः देवर्षिपितॄणां गणाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhatūryamṛdaṅgādyā nedurdundubhayo divitatra sarva upājagmuḥ devarṣipitṝṇāṃ gaṇāḥ
translation: The heavens were reverberating with sounds of musical instruments like conchs, trumpets, timbrels and drums. There assembled all the gods, sages and pitṛs (manes) of all grades.

devanagari: ब्रह्मा जगद्‍गुरुर्देवैः सहासृत्य सुरेश्वरैः । वैन्यस्य दक्षिणे हस्ते दृष्ट्वा चिह्नं गदाभृतः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmā jagad‍gururdevaiḥ sahāsṛtya sureśvaraiḥvainyasya dakṣiṇe haste dṛṣṭvā cihnaṃ gadābhṛtaḥ
translation: With gods and kings of gods, Brahmā, the father of all the worlds, arrived there. Observing the mark of Viṣṇu (the wielder of the mace) on (Pṛthu’s) right palm, and the lotus-mark on the soles of Pṛthu’s feet he concluded that he (Pṛthu) is verily the part (kalā) of Viṣṇu; for he on whose palm the cakra (discus) mark is uncrossed by any other line, is the aṃśa (portion) of the Lord.

devanagari: पादयोः अरविन्दं च तं वै मेने हरेः कलाम् । यस्याप्रतिहतं चक्रं अंशः स परमेष्ठिनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: pādayoḥ aravindaṃ ca taṃ vai mene hareḥ kalāmyasyāpratihataṃ cakraṃ aṃśaḥ sa parameṣṭhinaḥ
translation: With gods and kings of gods, Brahmā, the father of all the worlds, arrived there. Observing the mark of Viṣṇu (the wielder of the mace) on (Pṛthu’s) right palm, and the lotus-mark on the soles of Pṛthu’s feet he concluded that he (Pṛthu) is verily the part (kalā) of Viṣṇu; for he on whose palm the cakra (discus) mark is uncrossed by any other line, is the aṃśa (portion) of the Lord.

devanagari: तस्याभिषेक आरब्धो ब्राह्मणैर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः । आभिषेचनिकान्यस्मै आजह्रुः सर्वतो जनाः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyābhiṣeka ārabdho brāhmaṇairbrahmavādibhiḥābhiṣecanikānyasmai ājahruḥ sarvato janāḥ
translation: His coronation ceremony was commenced by Brāhmaṇas who were well-versed in Vedas (and expert in the Vedic rites and rituals of the ceremony). From all directions, people brought all the materials necessary for the coronation ceremony.

devanagari: सरित्समुद्रा गिरयो नागा गावः खगा मृगाः । द्यौः क्षितिः सर्वभूतानि समाजह्रुरुपायनम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: saritsamudrā girayo nāgā gāvaḥ khagā mṛgāḥdyauḥ kṣitiḥ sarvabhūtāni samājahrurupāyanam
translation: Rivers, seas, mountains, snakes, cattle, birds, beasts, deer, the sky, the earth and all beings brought tributes to him.

devanagari: सोऽभिषिक्तो महाराजः सुवासाः साध्वलङ्कृतः । पत्‍न्यार्चिषालङ्कृतया विरेजेऽग्निरिवापरः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: so'bhiṣikto mahārājaḥ suvāsāḥ sādhvalaṅkṛtaḥpat‍nyārciṣālaṅkṛtayā vireje'gnirivāparaḥ
translation: When the emperor, arrayed in excellent royal robes, and richly adorned with ornaments, was thus installed, along with the well-adorned queen, consort Arcis, he shone like another Fire-god.

devanagari: तस्मै जहार धनदो हैमं वीर वरासनम् । वरुणः सलिलस्रावं आतपत्रं शशिप्रभम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai jahāra dhanado haimaṃ vīra varāsanamvaruṇaḥ salilasrāvaṃ ātapatraṃ śaśiprabham
translation: Oh warrior, Kubera (the god of wealth) brought to him an excellent throne of gold; Varuṇa (the Lord of Waters), a royal umbrella radiating moon-white rays and dripping with water;

devanagari: वायुश्च वालव्यजने धर्मः कीर्तिमयीं स्रजम् । इन्द्रः किरीटमुत्कृष्टं दण्डं संयमनं यमः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vāyuśca vālavyajane dharmaḥ kīrtimayīṃ srajamindraḥ kirīṭamutkṛṣṭaṃ daṇḍaṃ saṃyamanaṃ yamaḥ
translation: Vāyu (the Wind-god) presented him two chowries (special types of fans made of the tufts of hair on the tails of particular kinds of wild cows); Dharma, a garland, the very embodiment of glory; Indra (the King of gods), an excellent crown; Yama (the god of death and controller of the world), a sceptre to govern the world.

devanagari: ब्रह्मा ब्रह्ममयं वर्म भारती हारमुत्तमम् । हरिः सुदर्शनं चक्रं तत् पत्‍न्यव्याहतां श्रियम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmā brahmamayaṃ varma bhāratī hāramuttamamhariḥ sudarśanaṃ cakraṃ tat pat‍nyavyāhatāṃ śriyam
translation: God Brahmā bestowed upon him an armour of Vedic incantations (or Vedic knowledge);the goddess of learning, an excellent necklace; Lord Hari, the Sudarśana disc, and his consort Lakṣmī, undiminishing prosperity and affluence.

devanagari: दशचन्द्रमसिं रुद्रः शतचन्द्रं तथाम्बिका । सोमोऽमृतमयानश्वान् त्वष्टा रूपाश्रयं रथम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: daśacandramasiṃ rudraḥ śatacandraṃ tathāmbikāsomo'mṛtamayānaśvān tvaṣṭā rūpāśrayaṃ ratham
translation: Rudra gave him a sword engraved with ten moon-like marks, while Pārvatī, gave him a shield bearing the marks of a hundred moons; the Moon-god presented him immortal, indefatigable, undaunted horses made of nectar, while Tvaṣṭṛ gave him extremely artistic and beautiful chariot.

devanagari: अग्निराजगवं चापं सूर्यो रश्मिमयानिषून् । भूः पादुके योगमय्यौ द्यौः पुष्पावलिमन्वहम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: agnirājagavaṃ cāpaṃ sūryo raśmimayāniṣūnbhūḥ pāduke yogamayyau dyauḥ puṣpāvalimanvaham
translation: The Fire-god gave him a bow made from the horns of goats and oxen; the Sun-god supplied him with his solarray-like arrows. The earth presented him sandals with mystic (Yogic) powers; and the sky, an ever fresh flower garland.

devanagari: नाट्यं सुगीतं वादित्रं अन्तर्धानं च खेचराः । ऋषयश्चाशिषः सत्याः समुद्रः शङ्खमात्मजम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: nāṭyaṃ sugītaṃ vāditraṃ antardhānaṃ ca khecarāḥṛṣayaścāśiṣaḥ satyāḥ samudraḥ śaṅkhamātmajam
translation: The demigods that move through the sky bestowed on him the arts of dancing, vocal and instrumental music, and the mystic power of vanishing. The sages gave him their never-failing efficacious blessings, and the Ocean-god, a conch born from him.

devanagari: सिन्धवः पर्वता नद्यो रथवीथीर्महात्मनः । सूतोऽथ मागधो वन्दी तं स्तोतुमुपतस्थिरे ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sindhavaḥ parvatā nadyo rathavīthīrmahātmanaḥsūto'tha māgadho vandī taṃ stotumupatasthire
translation: The oceans, mountains and rivers gave the high- souled King roads of communications by chariots. Then bards (Sūtas) minstrels (Māgadhas) and heralds (Bandins) began to sing his panygeric.

devanagari: स्तावकान् तानभिप्रेत्य पृथुर्वैन्यः प्रतापवान् । मेघनिर्ह्रादया वाचा प्रहसन् इदमब्रवीत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: stāvakān tānabhipretya pṛthurvainyaḥ pratāpavānmeghanirhrādayā vācā prahasan idamabravīt
translation: Knowing their intention, Pṛthu, the chivalrous son of Vena, laughed and spoke to them in a voice deep like the thunder of clouds.

devanagari: पृथुरुवाच । भोः सूत हे मागध सौम्य वन्दिन् । लोकेऽधुनास्पष्टगुणस्य मे स्यात् । किमाश्रयो मे स्तव एष योज्यतां । मा मय्यभूवन् वितथा गिरो व...
english_devnagari: pṛthuruvācabhoḥ sūta he māgadha saumya vandinloke'dhunāspaṣṭaguṇasya me syātkimāśrayo me stava eṣa yojyatāṃmā mayyabhūvan vitathā giro vaḥ
translation:
Pṛthu said:
Oh Sūta! Oh Māgadha! Oh gentle Bandin! What is the basis of your panygeric of me whose qualities are yet to be manifested in the world? You should address it to me when my merits will be acknowledged by the public. Your words of praise in my case should not prove untrue.

devanagari: तस्मात्परोक्षेऽस्मदुपश्रुतान्यलं । करिष्यथ स्तोत्रमपीच्यवाचः । सत्युत्तमश्लोकगुणानुवादे । जुगुप्सितं न स्तवयन्ति सभ्याः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātparokṣe'smadupaśrutānyalaṃkariṣyatha stotramapīcyavācaḥsatyuttamaślokaguṇānuvādejugupsitaṃ na stavayanti sabhyāḥ
translation: Oh bards of sweet words! Therefore, after a lapse of some time (when we perform some exploits worth singing), you may compose as many panygerics about my glory as you like. But when praise-worthy attributes of Lord Hari of excellent glory are there for glorification, good people do not eulogise ordinary (contemptible) men (like me).

devanagari: महद्‍गुणानात्मनि कर्तुमीशः । कः स्तावकैः स्तावयतेऽसतोऽपि । तेऽस्याभविष्यन् इति विप्रलब्धो । जनावहासं कुमतिर्न वेद ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: mahad‍guṇānātmani kartumīśaḥkaḥ stāvakaiḥ stāvayate'sato'pite'syābhaviṣyan iti vipralabdhojanāvahāsaṃ kumatirna veda
translation: What man with the capability of acquiring high merits will allow the bards to sing of qualities that are actually non-existent in him? The dullard who fools himself that those (non-existent) virtues may be manifest in him (later), does not see that he becomes thereby a butt of public ridicule.

devanagari: प्रभवो ह्यात्मनः स्तोत्रं जुगुप्सन्त्यपि विश्रुताः । ह्रीमन्तः परमोदाराः पौरुषं वा विगर्हितम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: prabhavo hyātmanaḥ stotraṃ jugupsantyapi viśrutāḥhrīmantaḥ paramodārāḥ pauruṣaṃ vā vigarhitam
translation: Really competent persons and illustrious persons abhor the praise. (even though they possess the qualities so praised, and are known for them). Truly modest and noble-minded persons shrink from the praise of their uncomplimentary show of valour.

devanagari: वयं तु अविदिता लोके सूताद्यापि वरीमभिः । कर्मभिः कथमात्मानं गापयिष्याम बालवत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ tu aviditā loke sūtādyāpi varīmabhiḥkarmabhiḥ kathamātmānaṃ gāpayiṣyāma bālavat
translation:
Oh bard! We are still unknown in this world for the performance of any exploit. (As we have not performed any great deed which can be extolled), how can we, like fools, allow you to sing of our glory.
[back to top]
